text: dear eighteen-year-old Mandy stop trying
to be somebody else being Mandy is
enough I was always trying to be Mandy
the singer Amanda the actress Mandy the
bodybuilder it was never enough to just
be me
I always felt the need to be something
else for everybody else ignore what
other people think of you I would just
beat myself up inside constantly it was
things like they hate you they're
staring at you they don't like what
you're waiting I was just a mess and I
was ill I just didn't know I needed help
my relationship with my husband brought
me so much happiness but ultimately it
couldn't save me from my own thoughts
and destruction you go through a lot of
tragedy in a short space of time
my marriage had ended I was involved in
a fire my pet cat died in the fire joy
and I lost all of my belongings I had to
start again overnight your life will
change forever
I reached breaking point I felt totally
empty I felt like nobody saw me
I just wanted to disappear so I just
started running I started running
towards the bridge and I wanted to take
my own life there I was then admitted to
hospital I finally got the help I needed
I was diagnosed with borderline
personality disorder I started on
medication and I started writing a blog
and which kind of documented my road to
recovery
[Music]
it's become a real community a real
supportive community and part of the
kind of overall project as this exercise
to write a letter to eighteen year old
you and I found out a really really
therapeutic experience we've had
hundreds come in from lawyers doctors
students young mums all doing the same
exercise and all getting different
things from it I think that there's
something different for everybody to be
gained from sitting down and reflecting
and Rea into u18 some of the women
who've written to me get together and
take to the streets to share their own
messages of empowerment we hand out
flowers to strangers to help them feel
seen and empowered to if I can help
others with my own journey then what's
not to love
my best bit of advice to eat a little
brandy don't hate parts of yourself from
the way the world makes those parts
trust yourself enough to fly
[Music]
